info.title: HR Platform Integration API
info.description: API for external integrations with the HR platform. Covers employees, contracts, departments, payroll, time-off, documents, webhooks, SSO, and API key management.

post.description: Issues an access token using the OAuth2 client_credentials grant. Send client_id and client_secret in the request body. Tokens expire after 3600 seconds. All API endpoints require a valid Bearer token in the Authorization header. When a token expires the integration must request a new one — there is no refresh token in this flow. Proactively renew the token 60 seconds before expiry to avoid 401 errors. Each client_id has associated scopes; accessing a resource outside the granted scopes returns 403, not 401.

200.description: Access token issued with expires_in field

post.description: Immediately invalidates an active access token. Useful when rotating credentials or decommissioning an integration. Any request using the revoked token will receive 401.

401.description: Token not found or already revoked

get.description: Returns a paginated list of employees using offset-based pagination via page and page_size. Deprecated in favour of /v2/employees. Supports filtering by department_id, status (ACTIVE, PENDING, TERMINATED), and hire_date range.

post.description: Creates a new employee record in PENDING status. Transitions to ACTIVE after HR approval. The rut field is the Chilean national ID and must be unique. The contract_type field accepts INDEFINITE, FIXED_TERM, or PART_TIME.

get.description: Returns the full profile of a single employee including personal data, contract, department, position, and current status. Sensitive fields like base_salary are omitted unless the integration has the payroll:read scope.

put.description: Updates mutable fields of an employee. Immutable fields (rut, hire_date) are silently ignored. Partial updates supported — omitted fields retain current value.

delete.description: Initiates termination for an employee. Transitions to TERMINATED after termination_date. Requires termination_reason. Payroll cycles including this employee remain accessible after termination.

get.description: Returns employees using cursor-based pagination. Pass next_cursor from the previous response. Returns up to 100 records per page. Use updated_after to sync only employees modified since a given timestamp. Replaces /v1/employees for new integrations.

200.description: Cursor-paginated list of employees with next_cursor field

post.description: Transitions an employee from PENDING to ACTIVE status. This operation triggers an employee.created webhook event to all subscribers. Cannot be applied to already ACTIVE or TERMINATED employees.

409.description: Employee is not in PENDING status

get.description: Returns all documents attached to an employee, including contracts, ID copies, certificates, and payslips. Documents are returned as metadata with a signed download URL valid for 15 minutes.

200.description: List of documents with signed URLs

post.description: Attaches a document to an employee. Supported types: CONTRACT, ID_COPY, CERTIFICATE, OTHER. Files must be PDF or image. Maximum size 10MB. The response includes the document ID for future reference.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a document attached to an employee. This operation cannot be undone. Documents of type CONTRACT cannot be deleted if the employee has an active contract.

get.description: Returns all departments in the organization including their parent department (for hierarchical structures), cost center, and headcount.

post.description: Creates a new department. Optionally assign a parent_id to create a sub-department. The cost_center field is used for payroll reporting.

get.description: Returns a single department including its full hierarchy path, list of positions, manager, and current headcount.

put.description: Updates a department's mutable fields. Changing parent_id moves the department in the hierarchy. Moving a department does not affect the employees assigned to it.

delete.description: Deletes a department. Fails if the department has active employees or sub-departments. Reassign employees to another department before deleting.

409.description: Department has active employees or sub-departments

get.description: Returns all job positions (cargos) defined in the organization. Positions are templates that define the job title, salary band, and required skills for a role.

post.description: Creates a new job position. salary_band_min and salary_band_max define the compensation range for the role and are used in payroll validation.

get.description: Returns a single job position including salary band, assigned department, and list of employees currently holding this position.

put.description: Updates a job position. Updating salary_band_min or salary_band_max does not retroactively change existing employee salaries.

get.description: Returns all employment contracts. Each contract belongs to an employee and defines the contractual relationship including type, salary, and term. An employee can have only one ACTIVE contract at a time.

post.description: Creates a new employment contract for an employee. type must be INDEFINITE, FIXED_TERM, or PART_TIME. For FIXED_TERM contracts, end_date is required. Creating a new contract for an employee with an ACTIVE contract will set the previous one to SUPERSEDED.

409.description: Employee already has an active contract

get.description: Returns a single contract including salary history and amendment log.

put.description: Amends an active contract. Salary changes are effective from effective_date. Each amendment is recorded in the contract's amendment_log for audit purposes.

get.description: Returns all payroll cycles for the company. Each cycle represents a monthly payroll run with states OPEN, PROCESSING, CLOSED, or PAID. Settlement data is only available for CLOSED and PAID cycles.
Returns full details of a payroll cycle including gross totals, net totals, employer contributions, and cycle metadata. Check cycle status before requesting settlements.

get.summary: List settlements for a payroll cycle
get.description: Returns the individual settlement (liquidacion) for each employee in the cycle. Only available when cycle status is CLOSED or PAID. Each settlement includes gross_salary, deductions, net_salary, AFP contributions, and health contributions.

409.description: Cycle is not CLOSED or PAID

get.description: Returns the full settlement detail for one employee in a cycle, including gross_salary, each deduction line item, net_salary, AFP institution, AFP contribution amount, health institution, health contribution amount, and any one-time bonuses or deductions.
Returns a signed URL to download the settlement PDF (liquidacion de sueldo) for one employee. The URL expires after 15 minutes. Requires payroll:read scope.

200.description: Signed URL to PDF valid for 15 minutes

get.description: Returns variable compensation items (bonuses, commissions, overtime) pending for the next cycle. Variables can be created by integrations and are included in the next OPEN cycle.

post.description: Submits a variable compensation item for inclusion in the next payroll cycle. type must be BONUS, COMMISSION, OVERTIME, or DEDUCTION. amount must be positive for BONUS/COMMISSION/OVERTIME and will be treated as a deduction if type is DEDUCTION.

delete.description: Cancels a pending payroll variable before it is included in a cycle. Only variables in PENDING status can be cancelled. Once a cycle moves to PROCESSING the variables are locked.

409.description: Variable is already locked in a cycle

get.description: Returns time-off requests (vacaciones, licencias, permisos). Filter by employee, status (PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED), or date range. Includes the reason, requested days, and approver.

post.description: Submits a time-off request on behalf of an employee. type can be VACATION, SICK_LEAVE, PERSONAL_LEAVE, or MATERNITY_PATERNITY. Requests are created in PENDING status and must be approved by a manager.

201.description: Time-off request created in PENDING status

get.description: Returns a single time-off request including approval history and the number of working days consumed.

delete.description: Cancels a PENDING or APPROVED time-off request. Cancelling an APPROVED request in the past returns the consumed days to the employee's balance.

409.description: Request is already REJECTED or in the past

get.description: Returns the current vacation and leave balance for one or more employees. Balance is expressed in working days. Includes accrued, used, and remaining days broken down by leave type.
Returns a headcount summary grouped by department, position, or contract type. Includes active, pending, and terminated employees as of the reference date.

Returns aggregated payroll data for a given cycle or date range. Includes total gross, total net, total employer cost, AFP contributions, and health contributions grouped by department or cost center.
Returns employee turnover metrics for a given period: hires, terminations, and turnover rate. Breakdowns available by department and termination reason.

post.description: Triggers an async export of a report. Supported report types: EMPLOYEES, PAYROLL, CONTRACTS, TIME_OFF. Supported formats: CSV, XLSX. The response includes a job_id. Poll /v1/reports/export/{job_id} to check status and get the download URL.

202.description: Export job created — poll job_id for status

get.description: Returns the status of an export job. status can be PENDING, PROCESSING, COMPLETED, or FAILED. When COMPLETED, the response includes a signed download URL valid for 60 minutes.

200.description: Export job status with download URL when completed

get.description: Returns all webhook subscriptions for the integration including target URL, subscribed events, active status, and last delivery result.

post.description: Registers a new webhook subscription. Available events: employee.created, employee.updated, employee.terminated, contract.created, contract.amended, payroll.cycle.closed, payroll.cycle.paid, time_off.approved, time_off.rejected. The secret is used to compute an HMAC-SHA256 signature sent in the X-Signature header on each delivery.

400.description: Invalid URL or unknown event type

get.description: Returns a single webhook subscription including delivery history for the last 100 events with HTTP status codes and response times.

put.description: Updates a webhook subscription. You can change the URL, event list, active status, or rotate the secret. Rotating the secret takes effect immediately on the next delivery.

delete.description: Removes a webhook subscription. Deliveries stop immediately. In-flight deliveries already queued may still arrive within 60 seconds.

post.description: Sends a synthetic test payload to the webhook URL to verify connectivity and signature validation. The delivery is marked as TEST in the delivery history.

200.description: Test delivery sent — check delivery history for result

get.description: Returns all API keys for the integration. The full key value is never returned after creation — only the key ID, name, prefix, and expiry are shown.

post.description: Creates a new API key. The full key value is returned only once in this response — store it securely. API keys have narrower scope than OAuth2 tokens and should only be used for low-risk sandbox endpoints such as webhook test triggers.

201.description: API key created — full value returned only here

delete.description: Permanently revokes an API key. Any subsequent request using this key receives a 401 immediately. For key rotation, create the new key before revoking the old one.

post.description: Uploads the SAML 2.0 identity provider metadata to configure SSO for the organization. Supported providers: Okta, Azure AD, Google Workspace, and any SAML 2.0 compliant IdP. Users authenticate via SSO and are auto-provisioned on first login if auto_provision is true.

400.description: Invalid metadata or unreachable metadata URL

get.description: Returns the current SAML configuration including IdP entity ID, ACS URL, last successful SSO login, and whether auto-provisioning is enabled.

404.description: SSO not configured for this organization

post.description: Disables SAML SSO for the organization. Users will fall back to password authentication. All active SSO sessions are invalidated immediately.

get.description: Returns all roles available for assignment to integrations. Each role defines a set of scopes. Roles cannot be created via API — they are managed by the platform administrator.

200.description: List of roles with their scopes

get.description: Returns all scopes granted by a specific role. Scopes follow the pattern resource:action (e.g. employees:read, payroll:write). An integration must have the required scope to call a protected endpoint, otherwise it receives 403.

Returns audit log entries for actions performed via the API. Each entry includes the timestamp, actor (integration client_id), resource type, resource ID, action, and IP address. Useful for compliance and debugging.

Returns the current rate limit counters for the authenticated integration: requests used, requests remaining, and reset timestamp for each limit window (per-second, per-minute, per-hour). Useful for implementing adaptive backoff.

200.description: Rate limit counters and reset timestamps

get.description: Returns all cost centers defined in the organization. Cost centers are used to group payroll expenditure for accounting and reporting purposes. Each department can be linked to a cost center.

post.description: Creates a new cost center. The code must be unique and is used as the GL account identifier in payroll exports.

get.description: Returns a single cost center including the list of departments linked to it and the total headcount and payroll cost for the current open cycle.

put.description: Updates a cost center name or description. The code cannot be changed after creation.

delete.description: Deletes a cost center. Fails if any department is currently linked to it.

get.description: Returns the bank accounts registered for payroll deposit for an employee. Multiple accounts are supported. The primary account receives the net salary transfer each cycle.

post.description: Adds a bank account for payroll deposit. bank must be a valid Chilean bank code (e.g. BCI, SANTANDER, BICE, ESTADO). account_type can be CHECKING or SAVINGS.

delete.description: Removes a bank account from an employee. Cannot remove the primary account if it is the only account. Assign another account as primary first.

409.description: Cannot remove the only primary account

get.description: Returns the emergency contacts registered for an employee. Used for HR compliance and occupational safety records.

post.description: Adds an emergency contact for an employee. relationship describes the contact's relation to the employee (e.g. SPOUSE, PARENT, SIBLING).

Transfers an employee to a new department and/or position effective on transfer_date. The previous department and position are recorded in the employee's history. If the new position has a different salary band, a contract amendment is required separately.

get.description: Returns clock-in and clock-out records for one or more employees. Used for overtime calculation and integration with time-tracking systems. Records include the source (MANUAL, BIOMETRIC, MOBILE_APP).

post.description: Submits a clock-in or clock-out event for an employee. type must be CLOCK_IN or CLOCK_OUT. The timestamp must not be more than 7 days in the past. Duplicate CLOCK_IN without an intervening CLOCK_OUT is rejected.

409.description: Duplicate event or timestamp out of range

delete.description: Deletes a single attendance record. Only records with source MANUAL can be deleted via API. BIOMETRIC and MOBILE_APP records can only be deleted by an HR administrator.

403.description: Cannot delete non-MANUAL records via API

get.description: Returns an aggregated attendance summary per employee for a period, including total hours worked, overtime hours, and days absent. Used as input for payroll overtime calculations.
Returns all active notification subscriptions for the integration. Unlike webhooks, notifications are delivered via email or Slack and do not require a public HTTPS endpoint.

post.description: Creates a notification subscription for specific events. delivery_channel must be EMAIL or SLACK. For SLACK, provide the webhook_url. Available events mirror the webhook event list.

delete.description: Removes a notification subscription. The integration stops receiving notifications for the subscribed events on this channel.

get.description: Returns payroll corrections (reliquidaciones) applied to past cycles. A correction adjusts a previously PAID settlement, for example to include a retroactive salary increase or fix a calculation error.

post.description: Submits a payroll correction (reliquidacion) for a previously PAID cycle. The correction is included in the next OPEN cycle as a retroactive adjustment. Requires payroll:write scope. The mandatory reason field is recorded in the audit log.

409.description: Original cycle is not in PAID status

get.description: Returns the full organizational hierarchy as a tree structure. Each node includes department ID, name, manager employee ID, headcount, and list of child department IDs. Useful for rendering org charts in external tools.
Returns the reporting structure for one or more employees: direct manager, direct reports, and peers within the same department. Useful for building org views in external tools.

Returns a health status for the integration: authentication validity, remaining quota, any suspended scopes, and whether any configured webhooks have been auto-disabled due to repeated delivery failures.
Returns the current configuration of the integration: display name, logo URL, allowed redirect URIs, assigned role, and enabled scopes.

put.description: Updates integration display metadata such as name, logo URL, and allowed redirect URIs. Scope and role changes must be requested through the marketplace portal.
